1.texto_original: ALLORA il profeta Aggeo, e Zaccaria, figliuolo d’Iddo, profeti, ch’erano mandati a’ Giudei ch’erano in Giuda e in Gerusalemme, profetizzarono loro a Nome dell’Iddio d’Israele.
1.normal:
Devotional Study of Esdra 5:1

"ALLORA il profeta Aggeo, e Zaccaria, figliuolo d’Iddo, profeti, ch’erano mandati a’ Giudei ch’erano in Giuda e in Gerusalemme, profetizzarono loro a Nome dell’Iddio d’Israele."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
1.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of Esdra 5:1

Philological Analysis

"ALLORA il profeta Aggeo, e Zaccaria, figliuolo d’Iddo, profeti, ch’erano mandati a’ Giudei ch’erano in Giuda e in Gerusalemme, profetizzarono loro a Nome dell’Iddio d’Israele."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
1.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"ALLORA il profeta Aggeo, e Zaccaria, figliuolo d’Iddo, profeti, ch’erano mandati a’ Giudei ch’erano in Giuda e in Gerusalemme, profetizzarono loro a Nome dell’Iddio d’Israele."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

"Ma l’anno primo di Ciro, re di Babilonia, egli ordinò che questa Casa di Dio fosse riedificata."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

14.texto_original: Ed anche il re Ciro trasse fuor del tempio di Babilonia i vasellamenti d’oro e d’argento della Casa di Dio, i quali Nebucadnesar avea tratti fuor del Tempio, ch’era in Gerusalemme, e portati nel tempio di Babilonia; e quelli furon dati ad uno, nominato Sesbassar, il quale Ciro avea costituito governatore.
14.normal:
Devotional Study of Esdra 5:14

"Ed anche il re Ciro trasse fuor del tempio di Babilonia i vasellamenti d’oro e d’argento della Casa di Dio, i quali Nebucadnesar avea tratti fuor del Tempio, ch’era in Gerusalemme, e portati nel tempio di Babilonia; e quelli furon dati ad uno, nominato Sesbassar, il quale Ciro avea costituito governatore."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
14.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of Esdra 5:14

Philological Analysis

"Ed anche il re Ciro trasse fuor del tempio di Babilonia i vasellamenti d’oro e d’argento della Casa di Dio, i quali Nebucadnesar avea tratti fuor del Tempio, ch’era in Gerusalemme, e portati nel tempio di Babilonia; e quelli furon dati ad uno, nominato Sesbassar, il quale Ciro avea costituito governatore."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.

17.texto_original: Ora dunque, se piace al re, ricerchisi costì nella casa de’ tesori del re, che è in Babilonia, se egli è vero che da Ciro fosse fatto comandamento che questa Casa di Dio fosse riedificata in Gerusalemme; e mandici il re la sua volontà intorno a ciò.
